audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar Midian-war, captives, purification, and spoil-division text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for vengeance-war command, Phinehas and trumpets, killing of Midianite males and kings, Balaam's death, captive women and children, Moses' further killing command, retained girls, corpse purification, metal purification, spoil division, tribute, and officers' gold memorial. Maximum anti-genocide, anti-child-killing, anti-sexual-violence, anti-xenophobia, and anti-plunder safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Avenge vengeance of children of Israel upon Midianites; afterward you shall be gathered to your people.
Moses spoke to people, saying, Arm men from among you for host, and let them be against Midian to give vengeance of YHWH upon Midian.
One thousand from each tribe, from all tribes of Israel, you shall send to host.
From thousands of Israel were delivered one thousand from each tribe, twelve thousand armed for war.
Moses sent them, one thousand from each tribe, to host, them and Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, to host, with vessels of holy place and trumpets for alarm in his hand.
They warred against Midian as YHWH commanded Moses, and they killed every male.
They killed kings of Midian with their slain: Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba, five kings of Midian; and Balaam son of Beor they killed with sword.
Children of Israel took captive women of Midian and their little ones, and all their cattle, all their livestock, and all their wealth they plundered.
All their cities in their settlements and all their encampments they burned with fire.
They took all spoil and all prey, human and beast,
and brought captives, prey, and spoil to Moses and Eleazar the priest and congregation of children of Israel, to camp at plains of Moab by Jordan at Jericho.
Moses and Eleazar the priest and all leaders of congregation went out to meet them outside camp.
Moses became angry with officers of host, commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, who came from service of war.
Moses said to them, Have you kept every female alive?
Look, these, through counsel of Balaam, were for children of Israel to act unfaithfully against YHWH in matter of Peor, and plague came among congregation of YHWH.
Now kill every male among little ones, and every woman who has known a man by lying with a male, kill.
But every female among little ones who has not known lying with a male, keep alive for yourselves.
You, camp outside camp seven days; everyone who killed a soul/life and everyone who touched slain, purify yourselves on third day and seventh day, you and your captives.
Every garment, every vessel of skin, everything made of goat hair, and every vessel of wood, you shall purify.
Eleazar the priest said to men of host who had gone to war, This is statute of Torah/instruction that YHWH commanded Moses:
Only gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, and lead,

everything that can enter fire, you shall make pass through fire and it shall be clean; yet with water of impurity it shall be purified. Everything that cannot enter fire you shall make pass through water.
You shall wash your garments on seventh day and be clean; afterward you may enter camp.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Lift up head/count of prey taken, human and beast, you and Eleazar the priest and heads of fathers' houses of congregation.
Divide prey into halves between those who handled war, who went out to host, and all congregation.
Levy tribute for YHWH from men of war who went out to host: one soul/life from five hundred, from humans, cattle, donkeys, and flock.
From their half you shall take it and give it to Eleazar the priest as contribution of YHWH.

From half belonging to children of Israel you shall take one taken from fifty, from humans, cattle, donkeys, flock, and every beast, and give them to Levites who keep charge of dwelling-place of YHWH.
Moses and Eleazar the priest did as YHWH commanded Moses.
The prey, remainder of spoil that people of host plundered, was six hundred seventy-five thousand flock,
seventy-two thousand cattle,
sixty-one thousand donkeys,
and human souls/lives, from women who had not known lying with a male, all souls/lives thirty-two thousand.
The half, portion of those who went out to host, number of flock was three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred.
Tribute to YHWH from flock was six hundred seventy-five.
Cattle were thirty-six thousand, and their tribute to YHWH seventy-two.
Donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred, and their tribute to YHWH sixty-one.
Human souls/lives were sixteen thousand, and their tribute to YHWH thirty-two souls/lives.
Moses gave tribute, contribution of YHWH, to Eleazar the priest, as YHWH commanded Moses.
From half belonging to children of Israel, which Moses divided from men who warred—
the congregation's half from flock was three hundred thirty-seven thousand five hundred,
cattle thirty-six thousand,
donkeys thirty thousand five hundred,
and human souls/lives sixteen thousand—
from half belonging to children of Israel Moses took one taken from fifty, from human and beast, and gave them to Levites keeping charge of dwelling-place of YHWH, as YHWH commanded Moses.
Officers over thousands of host, commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds, came near to Moses.
They said to Moses, Your servants have lifted up head/count of men of war who are in our hand, and not one man from us is missing.
We have brought offering of YHWH, each man what he found: vessels of gold, armlets and bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and beads, to make atonement/covering for our souls/lives before YHWH.
Moses and Eleazar the priest took gold from them, every crafted vessel.
All gold of contribution they lifted up to YHWH was sixteen thousand seven hundred fifty shekels, from commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds.
Men of host had plundered, each man for himself.
Moses and Eleazar the priest took gold from commanders of thousands and hundreds and brought it into tent of meeting as memorial for children of Israel before YHWH.

translation_notes: Numbers 31 is a conquest-and-captive narrative of extreme moral danger. Its commands to kill males, boys, and sexually experienced women must be preserved without euphemism and categorically rejected as models for modern conduct.

Verse 18 retains girls described as not having known a male 'for yourselves.' The text does not explain their future treatment; histories of captivity make sexual exploitation a grave concern that must not be minimized.
Women are blamed through the Peor interpretation, but Israelite men remain moral agents and Balaam is named as counselor. Women and foreign peoples must not become collective scapegoats.
Human captives are counted and divided alongside animals and goods. This dehumanizing structure must not be normalized as timeless Divine economics.
Corpse and object purification acknowledges contamination after killing but does not morally cleanse genocide, enslavement, sexual violence, or plunder.
The officers' claim that no Israelite soldier was missing and their gold memorial reflects the victors' perspective; it does not erase Midianite suffering.
Midianite identity in this chapter must not be mapped onto modern Arab, Muslim, Indigenous, or other populations.
safety_note: Never use Numbers 31 to justify genocide, child killing, enslavement, captive marriage, sexual violence, ethnic cleansing, religious war, collective punishment, or looting. This chapter must be received as severe ancient war material under categorical moral protest, with the humanity of Midianite women, children, and communities kept visible.
